title: The Cartographers of Lost Time
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In a world where maps can reveal lost moments from history, a disgraced cartographer discovers a continent that should not exist.
long_description:
In the city of Velar, cartography is not the study of places but of moments. The Cartographers' Guild creates maps that reveal the temporal residue of landscapes — echoes of battles, fragments of forgotten conversations, the shadow of a kiss left on the air for centuries. Elara Venn was the youngest master cartographer in Guild history until a catastrophic misreading destroyed a district and cost her everything.

Seven years later, a stranger brings her a fragment of an old map. The ink is not of any known origin. The coordinates lead to a place between known latitudes. When Elara touches the parchment, she sees a mountain range rising from a sea that dried up before mammals walked the Earth. The maps shows a time that predates the Guild, predates human civilization, predates the continents themselves. And something is looking back from that ancient emptiness.
